question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

answer: I want to start by pairing on my next ticket so I can see the approval bar in practice.

assessment.note: He names pairing on the next ticket to see the approval bar in practice, which signals a strong current clarity gap and some proactive engagement.

history.answer_excerpt: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
I want to start by pairing on my next ticket so I can see the approval bar in practice.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
I still feel fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
If we define what good looks like for my next two tickets, I will feel much steadier.
